pr.title: Bump dtk to v1.0.0 and objdiff-cli to v2.2.0
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Updated core decomp tooling pins: decomp-toolkit from 0.9.6 to 1.0.0, objdiff-cli from v2.1.0 to v2.2.0, and the Nix MWCC compiler archive to compilers_20240706. The Nix package hashes and Cargo lock output hashes were adjusted, and an old mwldeppc.exe postPatch workaround for random wibo crashes was removed because the newer compiler archive presumably includes the fixed artifact. No PR body, reviews, or comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: In .nix/decomp-toolkit.nix, changed the decomp-toolkit package version from 0.9.6 to 1.0.0 and updated the fetchFromGitHub source hash.
In .nix/decomp-toolkit.nix, kept the cargoLock entry for ar-0.8.0 but removed explicit outputHashes for objdiff-core-2.0.0-alpha.3 and ppc750cl-0.3.0.
In .nix/mwcc.nix, changed the compiler archive URL from compilers_20230715.zip to compilers_20240706.zip and updated the fetchzip sha256.

In .nix/mwcc.nix, removed the postPatch that wrote byte 0x51 into GC/1.1/mwldeppc.exe at offset 130933. The removed comment said this patch fixed the 1.1 linker reading garbage from the stack and causing random crashes under wibo.
In configure.py, updated config.dtk_tag from v0.9.6 to v1.0.0 and config.objdiff_tag from v2.1.0 to v2.2.0.
smart_moves: Kept the Nix package definition and configure.py tool version tags in sync, reducing the chance that local/Nix builds and configured non-Nix tooling use different dtk or objdiff versions.
Removed stale Cargo vendor output hash overrides instead of carrying unnecessary pinning forward after the dtk 1.0.0 upgrade.
Moved from a patched compiler binary to a newer compiler archive, avoiding a local binary mutation workaround in .nix/mwcc.nix.
Updated source hashes alongside version pins, making the dependency bump reproducible for Nix users.
decomp_lessons: When bumping decomp-toolkit, update both the Nix package version/hash and configure.py's config.dtk_tag together.
When bumping objdiff-cli for the project workflow, update configure.py's config.objdiff_tag even if the visible Nix diff is focused on decomp-toolkit.
Compiler archive updates can obsolete local binary patch workarounds; remove such patches when the upstream/archive artifact has advanced enough to make them unnecessary.
Nix Rust package bumps may require revisiting cargoLock.outputHashes because vendored Git dependencies or package versions can change or disappear.
naming_conventions: Tool version fields in configure.py use config.<tool>_tag naming, such as config.dtk_tag, config.objdiff_tag, config.compilers_tag, config.binutils_tag, config.sjiswrap_tag, and config.wibo_tag.
decomp-toolkit Nix packaging uses pname = "decomp-toolkit" and rev = "v${version}".
The MWCC compiler bundle derivation is named "GC_WII_COMPILERS" and fetches archives named compilers_YYYYMMDD.zip from files.decomp.dev.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching or C decompilation tactics were present; this PR was a toolchain/configuration bump.
The removed mwldeppc.exe byte patch is relevant to tool reliability rather than matching: it patched the 1.1 linker to avoid reading garbage from the stack and crashing under wibo.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that depend on decomp-toolkit v1.0.0 behavior or objdiff-cli v2.2.0 output.
Search for references to compilers_20240706 to see if the compiler archive update required downstream matching or build changes.
Check whether any documentation or setup scripts outside configure.py also pin dtk, objdiff, or MWCC versions.
Investigate why objdiff-core-2.0.0-alpha.3 and ppc750cl-0.3.0 Cargo output hashes were no longer needed after the dtk bump.
